documentation.xhtml: Is the quantity being measured? (Can be false e.g. if the sensor is deliberately not connected (yet).)
Gain to be applied to the measured value (default: 1 = no gain)
Offset to be added to the measured value (default: 0 = no offset)
Is the process enabled or not?
Is the process busy or not?
Is the process in a healthy state or not?
Is the process started or not?
Is the process step enabled or not?
New position to be taken over by the encoder
Deceleration (use 0 for default). If non zero, then also jerk must be non zero!
Jerk (use 0 for default). If non zero, then also deceleration must be non zero!
True to enable, false to disable
True to probe the rising edge, false to probe the falling edge
Timeout, in seconds. After this timeout, probing is aborted. timeout <= 0 means endless probing.
Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)
TRUE if the 'open' limit switch is active
TRUE if the 'open' limit switch is active
Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)

ST.xhtml: idle := (NOT(isBusy)) AND superState; busy := isBusy AND superState;

documentation.xhtml: Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)

ST.xhtml: started := isStarted AND superState; notStarted := (NOT(isStarted)) AND superState;

documentation.xhtml: Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)

ST.xhtml: enabled := isEnabled AND superState; disabled := (NOT(isEnabled)) AND superState;

documentation.xhtml: Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)

ST.xhtml: enabled := isEnabled AND superState; disabled := (NOT(isEnabled)) AND superState;

documentation.xhtml: Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)
TRUE if the subject is in Good health
TRUE to add a warning to the Good health state
The subject is in Good health
The subject is in Good health, but there are one or more warnings present
The subject is in Bad health
Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)
Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)
Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)
Value in the correct units to compare to the values from the config
The value is within normal range
Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)
Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)
Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)

ST.xhtml: auto := (state = OperatingStates.AUTO) AND superState; manual := (state = OperatingStates.MANUAL) AND superState;

documentation.xhtml: Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)

ST.xhtml: local := (state = OperatingModeStates.LOCAL) AND superState; remote := (state = OperatingModeStates.REMOTE) AND superState;

documentation.xhtml: Super state (TRUE if the super state is active, or if there is no super state)
Name of this function block instance
Buffer to write all logging to
Temporary buffer to write logging by parts (sub-statemachines) to

ST.xhtml: LOGGER( name := name, actualStatus := THIS^.actualStatus, previousStatus := THIS^.previousStatus, buffer := buffer, subBuffer := subBuffer);

documentation.xhtml: Name of this function block instance
Buffer to write all logging to
Temporary buffer to write logging by parts (sub-statemachines) to

ST.xhtml: LOGGER( name := name, actualStatus := THIS^.actualStatus, previousStatus := THIS^.previousStatus, buffer := buffer, subBuffer := subBuffer);

Buffer to write all logging to
Temporary buffer to write logging by parts (sub-statemachines) to

ST.xhtml: LOGGER( name := name, actualStatus := THIS^.actualStatus, previousStatus := THIS^.previousStatus, buffer := buffer, subBuffer := subBuffer);

Angular velocity in radians per second
Angular velocity in degrees per second
Angular velocity in arcseconds per second

Buffer to write all logging to
Temporary buffer to write logging by parts (sub-statemachines) to

ST.xhtml: LOGGER( name := name, actualStatus := THIS^.actualStatus, previousStatus := THIS^.previousStatus, buffer := buffer, subBuffer := subBuffer);

Name of this function block instance
Buffer to write all logging to
Temporary buffer to write logging by parts (sub-statemachines) to

ST.xhtml: LOGGER( name := name, actualStatus := THIS^.actualStatus, previousStatus := THIS^.previousStatus, buffer := buffer, subBuffer := subBuffer);

documentation.xhtml: Write TRUE to request the start of the process
Name of this function block instance
Buffer to write all logging to
Temporary buffer to write logging by parts (sub-statemachines) to

ST.xhtml: LOGGER( name := name, actualStatus := THIS^.actualStatus, previousStatus := THIS^.previousStatus, buffer := buffer, subBuffer := subBuffer, pHealthStatus := ADR(THIS^.statuses.healthStatus), pBusyStatus := ADR(THIS^.statuses.busyStatus));

documentation.xhtml: Error ID according to Beckhoff/PlcOpen Motion Control

ST.xhtml: SUPER^();

documentation.xhtml: The process corresponding to the step

ST.xhtml: statuses.enabledStatus( isEnabled := isEnabled );

documentation.xhtml: Name of this function block instance
Buffer to write all logging to
Temporary buffer to write logging by parts (sub-statemachines) to

ST.xhtml: LOGGER( name := name, actualStatus := THIS^.actualStatus, previousStatus := THIS^.previousStatus, buffer := buffer, subBuffer := subBuffer);

documentation.xhtml: Error ID according to Beckhoff XML Data Server library

ST.xhtml: SUPER^();

documentation.xhtml: At least 1 variable needed because subclass members of an empty class are not exposed by OPC UA (TwinCAT bug!)

Arguments in use by the process, if do_request was accepted
